results.title: Classification criteria for Sjögren's syndrome: a revised version of the European criteria proposed by the American-European Consensus Group
results.tldr: These classification criteria for Sjögren's syndrome have been re-examined by consensus group members, who have introduced some modifications, more clearly defined the rules for classifying patients with primary or secondary SS, and provided more precise exclusion criteria.

results.title: Sjögren's Syndrome
results.tldr: The term "Sjogren's disease" has been used as the condition appears to be a fairly clearly defined one, but until more is known about its aetiology and especially its relationship to the syndromes of Mikulicz and Heerfordt, it might be as well to avoid the term and to use the less specific expression of " Sjogen's syndrome.
results.abstract: The term "Sjogren's disease" has been used as the condition appears to be a fairly clearly defined one, but until more is known about its aetiology and especially its relationship to the syndromes of Mikulicz and Heerfordt, it might be as well to avoid the term and to use the less specific expression of " Sjogen's syndrome.

results.title: Preliminary criteria for the classification of Sjögren's syndrome. Results of a prospective concerted action supported by the European Community.
results.tldr: Using the findings of this prospective multicenter European study, general agreement can be reached on the diagnostic procedures to be used for patients with SS.

results.title: American College of Rheumatology Classification Criteria for Sjögren’s Syndrome: A Data-Driven, Expert Consensus Approach in the SICCA Cohort
results.tldr: New classification criteria for Sjögren's syndrome are proposed, which are needed considering the emergence of biologic agents as potential treatments and their associated comorbidity.

results.title: 2016 American College of Rheumatology/European League Against Rheumatism classification criteria for primary Sjögren's syndrome
results.tldr: A single set of data-driven consensus classification criteria for primary Sjögren's syndrome performed well in validation analyses and are well suited as criteria for enrolment in clinical trials.
results.abstract: A single set of data-driven consensus classification criteria for primary Sjögren's syndrome performed well in validation analyses and are well suited as criteria for enrolment in clinical trials.

results.title: Primary Sjögren's Syndrome
results.tldr: In the absence of trials involving patients with primary Sjögren's syndrome, severe organ manifestations are treated with immunosuppressive agents (including prednisone, methotrexate, mycophenolate sodium, azathioprine, and cyclophosphamide), in accordance with guidelines for systemic lupus erythematosus and other connective‐tissue diseases.
results.abstract: In the absence of trials involving patients with primary Sjögren's syndrome, severe organ manifestations are treated with immunosuppressive agents (including prednisone, methotrexate, mycophenolate sodium, azathioprine, and cyclophosphamide), in accordance with guidelines for systemic lupus erythematosus and other connective‐tissue diseases.

results.title: Epidemiology of primary Sjögren's syndrome: a systematic review and meta-analysis
results.tldr: A systematic review of the epidemiology of primary Sjögren's syndrome to assess the prevalence rates (PRs) and incidence rates (IRs), and to investigate possible geographic variations in pSS to better understand the global epidemiology.
results.abstract: A systematic review of the epidemiology of primary Sjögren's syndrome to assess the prevalence rates (PRs) and incidence rates (IRs), and to investigate possible geographic variations in pSS to better understand the global epidemiology.

results.title: Altered cytokine balance in the tear fluid and conjunctiva of patients with Sjögren's syndrome keratoconjunctivitis sicca.
results.tldr: The balance of cytokines in the tear fluid and conjunctival epithelium is altered in Sjögren's syndrome and new insight into the pathogenesis of keratoconjunctivitis is provided and potential targets for therapy are provided.
results.abstract: The balance of cytokines in the tear fluid and conjunctival epithelium is altered in Sjögren's syndrome and new insight into the pathogenesis of keratoconjunctivitis is provided and potential targets for therapy are provided.

results.title: Sjögren’s syndrome: a systemic autoimmune disease

results.title: The spectrum of benign to malignant lymphoproliferation in Sjögren's syndrome.
results.tldr: In Sjogren's syndrome, as in these other conditions, it seems likely that a combination of genetic, immunologic, and viral or other unknown environmental factors plays a role in pathogenesis.
results.abstract: In Sjogren's syndrome, as in these other conditions, it seems likely that a combination of genetic, immunologic, and viral or other unknown environmental factors plays a role in pathogenesis.

results.title: Improvement of Sjögren's syndrome after two infusions of rituximab (anti-CD20).
results.tldr: Low-dose rituximab infusions induced a rapid depletion of B cells in the blood and SG and could improve primary Sjögren's syndrome, and controlled studies are needed.
results.abstract: Low-dose rituximab infusions induced a rapid depletion of B cells in the blood and SG and could improve primary Sjögren's syndrome, and controlled studies are needed.

results.title: The geoepidemiology of Sjögren's syndrome.
results.tldr: Although the clinical manifestations of pSS patients are mainly those of an autoimmune exocrinopathy, almost half of patients develop extraglandular disease, which may be manifested either by epithelial lymphocytic invasion of lung, liver, or kidney (resulting in interstitial nephritis) or by skin vasculitis, peripheral neuropathy, glomerulonephritis, and low C4 levels.
results.abstract: Although the clinical manifestations of pSS patients are mainly those of an autoimmune exocrinopathy, almost half of patients develop extraglandular disease, which may be manifested either by epithelial lymphocytic invasion of lung, liver, or kidney (resulting in interstitial nephritis) or by skin vasculitis, peripheral neuropathy, glomerulonephritis, and low C4 levels.

results.title: Sjögren's syndrome: association of anti-Ro(SS-A) antibodies with vasculitis, hematologic abnormalities, and serologic hyperreactivity.
results.tldr: The presence of anti-Ro(SS-A) antibodies defines a subset of patients with Sjögren's syndrome who have systemic clinical manifestations including vasculitis, hematologic abnormalities, and serologic hyperreactivity.
results.abstract: The presence of anti-Ro(SS-A) antibodies defines a subset of patients with Sjögren's syndrome who have systemic clinical manifestations including vasculitis, hematologic abnormalities, and serologic hyperreactivity.

results.title: Revised Japanese criteria for Sjögren's syndrome (1999): availability and validity
results.tldr: To evaluate the validity of the revised criteria, the records of 900 patients, including SS patients and non-SS controls, from 54 clinical centers were registered and analyzed and the accuracy of the criteria was found to have 96.5% accuracy for diagnosing SS.
results.abstract: To evaluate the validity of the revised criteria, the records of 900 patients, including SS patients and non-SS controls, from 54 clinical centers were registered and analyzed and the accuracy of the criteria was found to have 96.5% accuracy for diagnosing SS.

results.title: Abnormal Distribution of Aquaporin-5 Water Channel Protein in Salivary Glands from Sjögren’s Syndrome Patients
results.tldr: Abnormal distribution of AQP5 in salivary gland acini is likely to contribute to the deficiency of fluid secretion, which is a defining feature of Sjögren’s syndrome.
results.abstract: Abnormal distribution of AQP5 in salivary gland acini is likely to contribute to the deficiency of fluid secretion, which is a defining feature of Sjögren’s syndrome.

results.title: Mortality and causes of death in primary Sjögren's syndrome: a prospective cohort study.
results.tldr: Hypocomplementemia, defined as C3 and/or C4 values in the lowest quartile of the SS patients' values at the time of diagnosis, was a significant predictor of death, mainly due to lymphoproliferative malignancy.
results.abstract: Hypocomplementemia, defined as C3 and/or C4 values in the lowest quartile of the SS patients' values at the time of diagnosis, was a significant predictor of death, mainly due to lymphoproliferative malignancy.

results.title: Neurologic Complications of Primary Sjögren's Syndrome
results.tldr: The high proportion of patients with concomitant peripheral vasculitis, and the observed association with antibodies to the Ro(SSA) antigen system, suggest that an immune vasculopathy may play a role in the pathogenesis of the CNS disease of SS.
results.abstract: The high proportion of patients with concomitant peripheral vasculitis, and the observed association with antibodies to the Ro(SSA) antigen system, suggest that an immune vasculopathy may play a role in the pathogenesis of the CNS disease of SS.

results.title: Primary Sjögren's syndrome with central nervous system disease mimicking multiple sclerosis.
results.tldr: Patients with primary Sjögren's syndrome and central nervous system involvement whose neurologic findings, evoked potential abnormalities, and cerebrospinal fluid profiles closely resembled those of multiple sclerosis were presented.
results.abstract: Patients with primary Sjögren's syndrome and central nervous system involvement whose neurologic findings, evoked potential abnormalities, and cerebrospinal fluid profiles closely resembled those of multiple sclerosis were presented.

results.title: Prevalence of serum and salivary antibodies to HTLV-1 in Sjögren's syndrome.
results.tldr: Seroepidemiological and virological studies conducted on patients with this syndrome in Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan suggest that HTLV-1 is involved in the pathogenesis of the disease in a subset of patients with Sjögren's syndrome in endemic areas.
results.abstract: Seroepidemiological and virological studies conducted on patients with this syndrome in Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan suggest that HTLV-1 is involved in the pathogenesis of the disease in a subset of patients with Sjögren's syndrome in endemic areas.

results.title: Hydroxychloroquine treatment for primary Sjögren's syndrome: a two year double blind crossover trial.
results.tldr: The use of hydroxychloroquine at a dose of 400 mg daily taken over a 12 month period does not have a worthwhile clinical benefit in patients with primary Sjögren's syndrome despite an improvement of hyperglobulinaemia and slight changes in the ESR and IgM.
results.abstract: The use of hydroxychloroquine at a dose of 400 mg daily taken over a 12 month period does not have a worthwhile clinical benefit in patients with primary Sjögren's syndrome despite an improvement of hyperglobulinaemia and slight changes in the ESR and IgM.

results.title: Renal involvement in primary Sjögren's syndrome: a clinicopathologic study.
results.venue: American Society of Nephrology. Clinical Journal
results.tldr: This case series supports chronic TIN as the predominant KB finding in patients with renal involvement from pSS and illustrates diverse glomerular lesions and should be considered in the clinical evaluation of kidney dysfunction in pSS.
results.abstract: This case series supports chronic TIN as the predominant KB finding in patients with renal involvement from pSS and illustrates diverse glomerular lesions and should be considered in the clinical evaluation of kidney dysfunction in pSS.

results.title: Shared gut, but distinct oral microbiota composition in primary Sjögren's syndrome and systemic lupus erythematosus.
results.tldr: pSS and SLE patients share similar alterations in gut microbiota composition, distinguishing patients from individuals in the general population, while oral microbiota composition shows disease-specific differences between pSS andSLE patients.
results.abstract: pSS and SLE patients share similar alterations in gut microbiota composition, distinguishing patients from individuals in the general population, while oral microbiota composition shows disease-specific differences between pSS andSLE patients.

results.title: Neuropsychiatric dysfunction in primary Sjögren's syndrome.
results.tldr: The diagnosis of primary Sjögren's syndrome should be considered in patients with unexplained neuropsychiatric illness, and a significant correlation between psychiatric disturbances and neurologic dysfunction, suggesting a possible organic basis for psychiatric dysfunction.
results.abstract: The diagnosis of primary Sjögren's syndrome should be considered in patients with unexplained neuropsychiatric illness, and a significant correlation between psychiatric disturbances and neurologic dysfunction, suggesting a possible organic basis for psychiatric dysfunction.

results.title: Sjögren's syndrome.

results.title: Safety and efficacy of subcutaneous ianalumab (VAY736) in patients with primary Sjögren's syndrome: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase 2b dose-finding trial.
results.tldr: This first large, randomised, controlled trial in primary Sjögren's syndrome met its primary objective, showing a dose-related decrease in disease activity as measured by ESSDAI at week 24, and there is potential for more studies of this mechanism in the future.

results.title: Gut dysbiosis is prevailing in Sjögren’s syndrome and is related to dry eye severity
results.tldr: Dysbiosis of the gut microbiota was partly correlated to dry eye severity, and Sjögren’s syndrome showed significant gut dysbiosis compared to controls and environmental dry eye syndrome, while dry eye patients showed compositional changes of gut microbiome somewhere in between Sj Ögren's syndrome and controls.

results.title: Current State of Knowledge on Primary Sjögren’s Syndrome, an Autoimmune Exocrinopathy

results.title: Sjögren's syndrome: Old and new therapeutic targets.

results.title: Pathogenesis and treatment of Sjogren’s syndrome: Review and update

results.title: A T cell intrinsic role of Id3 in a mouse model for primary Sjogren's syndrome.
results.tldr: It is shown that Id3-deficient mice develop many disease symptoms found in primary Sjogren's syndrome patients including dry eyes and mouth, lymphocyte infiltration in lachrymal and salivary glands, and development of anti-Ro and anti-La antibodies.

艾滋病患者的综合护理干预及并发症预防效果
results.abstract: <jats:p>目的：探讨综合护理干预对艾滋病患者并发症预防、生活质量提升的临床效果。方法：纳入2024年1月至2025年1月期间我院收治的80例艾滋病患者，采用随机数字表分为两组，对照组与观察组各40例，对照组采用常规护理干预，观察组采用综合护理干预，对比两组患者比较两组并发症发生率、生活质量、CD4?T淋巴细胞计数、病毒载量。结果：观察组CD4?T淋巴细胞计数高于对照组，病毒载量低于对照组，并发症发生率较对照组更低；观察组SAS、SDS评分低于对照组，生活质量评分高于对照组（P＜0.05）。结论：综合护理干预的应用可以降低艾滋病患者并发症的发生率，改善其负面情绪与免疫功能，提高患者生活质量，值得推荐。</jats:p>

results.title: 仔猪断奶应激综合征的营养干预策略及分子机制
results.abstract: <jats:p>仔猪断奶应激综合征(PWSS)是当前养殖业中一个极为突出的难题,它直接导致了断奶仔猪的生长速度显著减缓以及死亡率的大幅上升,成为制约养殖业发展的关键问题。其根本原因在于断奶过程中产生的应激反应,这种应激不仅引发了仔猪肠道屏障的严重损伤,还导致了免疫系统失衡以及代谢过程的紊乱。本文对PWSS的营养干预策略进行了全面而系统的综述,详细探讨了多种营养干预手段的应用机制。具体包括功能性氨基酸的应用,如谷氨酰胺和精氨酸在修复肠道屏障中的作用；益生菌与益生元的联合使用,如何通过调节肠道菌群来改善肠道健康；植物提取物的抗炎和抗氧化特性及其对仔猪免疫系统的调节作用；以及脂肪酸在维持肠道细胞结构和功能方面的关键作用。此外,文章还从分子层面深入解析了营养调控的具体作用机制,揭示了营养干预如何通过调控肠道紧密连接蛋白(如ZO-1、occludin)的表达,影响炎症信号通路(如NF-κB、MAPK)的激活,以及调节肠道菌群与宿主之间的互作网络,从而有效缓解断奶应激带来的负面影响。研究结果表明,精准的营养干预策略能够显著增强仔猪的肠道屏障功能,调节其免疫应答,并改善肠道微生态的平衡状态,从而有效

results.title: 干眼症患者门诊综合护理干预方案的构建与效果评价
results.abstract:
<jats:p>汤玲，李凌云*
南京医科大学第四附属医院，江苏南京
摘要：目的：研究干眼症患者门诊综合护理干预方案的构建与效果评价。方法：选取2024年7月至2025年7月收治的98例干眼症患者将其随机分为观察组和对照组，对照组采用干眼症常规门诊护理，观察组实施综合护理干预方案。比较两组眼部生理指标、护理总有效率的差异。结果：护理后，观察组泪液破裂时间（16.8±1.7）s、SchirmerⅠ泪液分泌量（15.1±1.2）mm均显著高于对照组（12.7±1.2）s、（13.1±0.9）mm，角膜荧光染色评分（0.3±0.2）分显著低于对照组（1.3±0.5）分，有统计学意义，P＜0.05；观察组临床总有效率为91.8%（45/49），显著高于对照组的75.5%（37/49），有统计学意义，P＜0.05。结论：针对干眼症门诊患者构建的综合护理干预方案，能有效改善患者的眼部生理指标，提升患者生活质量，具备临床推广价值，值得临床应用。</jats:p>

人性化护理干预对改善肾病综合症患者心理状况及睡眠状态的应用观察
results.abstract: <jats:p>为观察人性化护理干预对肾病综合征患者心理及睡眠的改善效果,选取2025年1—12月某市中心医院肾病内科180例患者,随机分为对照组与观察组各90例。对照组采用常规护理,观察组在其基础上加用人性化护理,干预3个月。以SAS、SDS量表评估心理状况,PSQI量表评估睡眠质量,并比较护理满意度。结果显示,干预前两组各项评分无统计学差异。结论表明,人性化护理可有效缓解患者焦虑抑郁情绪,改善睡眠质量,提升护理满意度,具有较高临床推广价值。</jats:p>

综合性妒理干预联合舒适性妒理在经皮椎闰孔镜治疗腰椎闰盘突出症申的应用
results.abstract: <jats:p>目的探讨在腰间盘突出患者中进行综合性护理干预联合舒适性护理的作用。方法 选取我院2017年6月至2018年6月入院治疗的80名腰间盘突出患者作为研究对象，经诊断都己确诊为CT或MRI，将两者根据入院 顺序平均分为两组，对照组、实验组，每组患者40名，通过比对两组患者的临床资料。结果实验组患者恢复情况明显高于对照组，两组患者临床数据没有明显差异，具有统计学意义。结论在腰间盘突出病症中应用综合性护理联合舒适性护理具有重要意义，但得广泛推广</jats:p>
